title: narcissus triandrus subsp. pallidulus
intro: Narcissus triandrus subsp. pallidulus is a subspecies of flowering plant in the Amaryllidaceae family, commonly known as the pale daffodil or pale narcissus. This plant has been used in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits associated with Narcissus triandrus subsp. pallidulus include its potential to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, as well as its ability to reduce fever and alleviate anxiety. Herbal preparations of this plant typically involve drying the flowers and using them in teas, infusions, or tinctures, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
